title: Worker that derives idempotency key from hashed args
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.EmailWorker` (queue `:mailers`, max_attempts 5) whose perform/1 takes `%{"to" => to, "subject" => subject, "body" => body}`. Before sending, compute an idempotency key as `:crypto.hash(:sha256, "#{to}|#{subject}|#{body}") |> Base.encode16(case: :lower)`. Look up `MyApp.EmailLog.by_key(key)` — if a record exists with `sent_at` set, return `:ok` (already sent). Otherwise insert a pending log entry with the key, send via `MyApp.Mailer.deliver/3`, then update the log entry with `sent_at: DateTime.utc_now()`. Wrap the insert/update in an Ecto.Multi so a crash after `deliver/3` still records the send. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/email_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Tests the 'idempotency-from-content' pattern with hashing + ledger. Realistic production approach.
